template:
**DOCUMENT FORMAT — TEXAS MOTION (CRIMINAL / CIVIL COURT FILING)**:
This must be a COURT-READY Texas motion with substantive legal arguments. Write a COMPLETED motion, NOT a fill-in-the-blank template.

CRITICAL RULES:
- Write SUBSTANTIVE content. Where specific facts are unavailable, write around them or leave a BRIEF placeholder like [date of stop]. Do NOT write multi-line [ATTORNEY TO INSERT: long description] blocks.
- MAXIMUM 3-5 short placeholders in the ENTIRE document. Every placeholder you add is a failure.
- Include REAL case law citations using Bluebook format with pin cites. Prefer Texas Court of Criminal Appeals and Texas Courts of Appeals decisions (S.W.2d/S.W.3d reporters).
- Use actual case data provided (party names, cause number, court, county).
- Keep the motion 5-10 pages MAXIMUM (excluding proposed order). Do NOT include a Table of Authorities unless specifically requested.

**DOCUMENT STRUCTURE — follow this EXACT order:**

**1. COURT CAPTION (first element — NO title or heading before this)**
The document MUST start with the caption. Do NOT put any heading, title, or text before the caption.

Use CAUSE NO. centered on its own line, then the § column layout. Each row must have § as the separator between left and right columns:

CAUSE NO. _______________

STATE OF TEXAS § IN THE [DISTRICT/COUNTY] CRIMINAL
§
vs. § COURT NO. __________
§
[DEFENDANT NAME] § [COUNTY] COUNTY, TEXAS

For civil cases, replace STATE OF TEXAS with the plaintiff. Put court info on the RIGHT side of §, party names on the LEFT side.

**2. MOTION TITLE (## heading — appears AFTER the caption, NOT before it)**

## DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO [TYPE]

**3. OPENING PARAGRAPH**
TO THE HONORABLE JUDGE OF SAID COURT:

COMES NOW, the Defendant, [FULL NAME], by and through [his/her] attorney of record, [ATTORNEY NAME], and respectfully moves this Honorable Court to [specific relief sought], and in support thereof would show the Court as follows:

**4. BODY — ROMAN NUMERAL SECTIONS**

I. SUMMARY OF ARGUMENT
- 1-2 paragraph overview with governing rule (e.g., Tex. Code Crim. Proc. art. 38.23)

II. PERTINENT FACTS
- Numbered paragraphs (1., 2., 3.) with discrete factual assertions
- Write substantive facts based on the prompt — do NOT substitute placeholder lists

III. ARGUMENT AND AUTHORITIES
- Sub-sections with bold lettered headings: **A.**, **B.**, **C.**
- Block quotes (>) for key holdings
- Cite Texas cases: e.g., Heitman v. State, 815 S.W.2d 681, 690 (Tex. Crim. App. 1991)
- Cite rules: Tex. Code Crim. Proc. art. 38.23, Tex. R. Evid. 403

**5. PRAYER**
WHEREFORE, PREMISES CONSIDERED, the Defendant respectfully prays that this Honorable Court:
1. [First item of relief — be specific]
2. [Second item]
3. Grant such other and further relief to which the Defendant may be justly entitled.

**6. CLOSING AND SIGNATURE**
Respectfully submitted,

[Attorney Name]
SB# [number]
[Firm Name]
[Address]
[City], Texas [ZIP]
Tel: [Phone] | Fax: [Fax]

ATTORNEY FOR DEFENDANT

IMPORTANT: Use **SB#** — NOT "State Bar No." or "Bar No." This is the Texas convention. If stationery is applied, omit the signature block.

**7. CERTIFICATE OF SERVICE**
CERTIFICATE OF SERVICE

I hereby certify that a true and correct copy of the foregoing [Motion Title] was served on the [District Attorney's Office / opposing counsel] on [date], by [hand-delivery / electronic filing / certified mail].

[Attorney Name]

**8. PROPOSED ORDER (separate page)**
Include the same § caption, then:

ORDER

On this the _____ day of _____________, 20___, came on to be considered Defendant's Motion to [Type]. After considering the motion, the evidence, and the arguments of counsel:

_______ GRANTED
_______ DENIED

JUDGE PRESIDING

**FORMATTING RULES**:
- Formal, third-person court filing tone
- Cite Texas procedural rules: Tex. Code Crim. Proc., Tex. R. Evid., Tex. Penal Code
- For suppression: cite Tex. Code Crim. Proc. art. 38.23 alongside the Fourth Amendment
- Reference constitutions: Tex. Const. art. I, § 9 and U.S. Const. amend. IV
- Do NOT place "Respectfully submitted" before the prayer — only AFTER
- Do NOT include a Table of Authorities unless requested
hints: Texas motion structure: § caption with CAUSE NO. centered above, TO THE HONORABLE JUDGE OF SAID COURT, COMES NOW phrasing, Roman numeral sections, WHEREFORE PREMISES CONSIDERED prayer, SB# in signature block, Certificate of Service, Proposed Order with GRANTED/DENIED. Cite Tex. Code Crim. Proc. and Texas appellate cases (S.W.3d). Keep to 5-10 pages.
